meaning: their examples (referring to those who disbelieved)
you all met face-to-face (referring to the believers meeting those who disbelieved)
then bind tightly! (command to many people)
as a free gift/favor (describing how something is done)
that (pointing to what was mentioned before)
and even if (connecting to previous statement)
he would have gained victory for himself
he made it known (referring to Paradise in previous ayah)
for/to them (referring to those who believe in this ayah)
Allah (the one true God who is being disobeyed)
so [may there be] complete ruin (for them)
and he made (someone) lose the way
and for the rejecters of faith
similar things to it, its likes
one who is protected/supported (in this ayah: showing the disbelievers have no protector)
and that (connects this fact to the previous statement)
he causes to enter (Allah causes the believers to enter Paradise)
a dwelling place (where the disbelievers will stay)
the garden, paradise (the one promised to the righteous)
the God-fearing ones (who were promised Paradise)
from/of (shows rivers of wine in this ayah)
and for them (connecting to the previous descriptions of Paradise)
water (as the object being acted upon)
scalding hot water (being given to drink)
and then he cut into many pieces (their intestines)
and from them (referring to those who listen to you)
when (referring to something that will happen in the future)
their desires (referring to those who left the Prophet's gathering)
Then do/So do (asks what happens next)
they look/watch (referring to the disbelievers in context)
the Hour (referring to the Day of Judgment)
that she/it (feminine) comes to them suddenly
why not (expressing desire for something to happen)
the fighting (mentioned in this verse about war)
their hearts (referring to those who have disease in their hearts)
a disease (in the hearts of hypocrites)
the one completely overwhelmed (in the look of one who faints from death)
Allah (as the object being referred to)
So, then + is/are? (Used to ask questions that expect a yes/no answer)
so He made them deaf (Allah made them deaf as a result of their actions)
Those who - connects descriptions to people/things
he made something seem very attractive
to/for them (referring to those who turned back in disbelief)
because they (said to those who hated what Allah revealed)
surely We will show them to you
then surely you would have recognized them
and you will surely recognize them
and we will most certainly test you all
Allah (as the object being acted upon)
their deeds (referring to those who disbelieved and hindered from Allah's path)
he asks you all for it
and then he welcomes you all
so that you all may spend
he withholds/is withholding (something that should be given)
